items.title: Enhance Dual-Write Connection Methods in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces dual-write connection methods with comprehensive method resolution and validation, ensuring robust integration across various provisioning paths. Key enhancements include the addition of method identifiers, execution types, and credential types, which are now exposed only after all writers are populated. The implementation has passed extensive testing, reinforcing its reliability and performance.

items.title: Enhance Agent Skill Manifest Copy in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces generated skill summaries with hand-written Chinese (zh-CN) copies for the Agent Workbench, improving clarity and user experience. Additionally, several skill artifact display names have been normalized, such as 'AI Chat' and 'HappyHorse Video'. All production agent manifests have been validated to ensure consistency and correctness.

items.title: Enhance Connector Methods Exposure in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces dual-write functionality for `Connector.connection_methods`, sourced from existing `auth_methods`. Key changes include the exposure of `connection_methods` and `recommended_method_id` in both public and internal serializers, while maintaining legacy fields for current consumers. The implementation ensures compatibility and prepares for future enhancements in connection creation paths.

items.title: Enhance Connection Methods with New Metadata in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces explicit connector and connection execution methods, enhancing the handling of runtime metadata. Key changes include the addition of new fields for connection types and improved routing for connector status and consent gates. The implementation ensures compatibility with existing AuthBackend setups while preparing for future enhancements.

items.title: Enhance Connector Method Preferences in Skills 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces canonical connector method preferences for Reddit and Weibo, prioritizing Reddit's official OAuth over cookie credentials while maintaining a fallback option. Weibo will continue to use cookie-only access until its OAuth support is implemented. The changes ensure that existing required-connection parsing remains intact and preference keys are now using canonical identifiers.

items.title: Enhance Connector Methods for Better Integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new execution and credential method types along with a centralized `getConnectorMethods()` adapter. The new `connection_methods` wire format is now preferred, ensuring backward compatibility with legacy `auth_methods`. All method-list consumers have been migrated to utilize the new adapter, enhancing the overall connection management experience.

items.title: Enhance Deployment Process and Boost SKALE Gas Limit in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a migration job that runs before deploying Facilitator pods, ensuring database migrations are applied successfully. Additionally, the SKALE gas limit has been increased from 400,000 to 2,000,000 to accommodate larger transactions. The deployment process now includes fail-safe mechanisms to abort if migration fails, enhancing overall reliability.

items.title: Clean Up Missing Agent Manifests in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes agent manifests for services that are not present in the production database, specifically aichat, face-change, and shorturl. The change ensures that the sync.py script operates fail-closed while allowing the verified production services to sync properly. This correction enhances the stability of the system by preventing issues related to unknown service aliases.

items.title: Enhance Multichain Settlement Process in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update strengthens the multichain settlement process by verifying Solana payer signatures and requiring exact EVM authorizations. It introduces new state transitions for settling and failed transactions, ensuring robust reconciliation of chain states without duplicates. Additionally, CI test coverage has been expanded to ensure reliability and performance.

items.title: Enhance SSR Deployment Readiness Check in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the SSR `kubectl rollout status` command with a more direct pod readiness and image validation approach. It also modifies the rolling update strategy to minimize capacity requirements during deployments. These changes aim to improve the reliability of SSR deployments and prevent issues with stale pods.

items.title: Enhance EVM and Solana Signing Features in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update strengthens the signing process for both EVM and Solana transactions. Key changes include the removal of clock-skew failures, the introduction of mandatory fee payers for Solana transactions, and alignment of types across Python and TypeScript. Additionally, new tests have been added to ensure robust functionality and compliance with recent requirements.

items.title: Introducing the Agent Workbench for Enhanced Document Management in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the previous document right-rail API-only section with a fully functional Agent Workbench. Key features include the addition of MCP and Skill panels driven by `document.agent` data, new typed models, and localized UI strings in multiple languages. The workbench is designed to align with the current dark code-surface documentation style and includes mobile-visible integration links.

items.title: Enhance Service Agent Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `Service.agent` declaration for machine-readable agent documentation, ensuring it is only exposed at the top level. It includes strict validation and the addition of 23 verified service manifests sourced from real MCP/Skills, enhancing the platform's capabilities. Key improvements also include refined localization and the replacement of unverified links with verified URLs.

items.title: In-depth LibTV vs Maestro Research Report in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive 673-line research report comparing LibTV and Maestro, detailing their workflows, growth models, and reliability metrics. Key recommendations include a phased approach to enhance reliability and asset management. The report has been registered in the active video-and-marketing plans index, ensuring it is accessible for future reference.

items.title: Enhance X402 Task Polling and Settlement in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several key enhancements to the X402 task polling and settlement process. It allows tokenless task retrieval to bypass payment for strict method matches, resolves task paths securely, and integrates Solana fee payer metadata. Additionally, it ensures proper authentication and pre-seeding of necessary secrets for production deployment.

items.title: Enhance Registration Throttling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a robust throttling mechanism for automated registrations and email code issuance. Key changes include rate-limiting based on independent buckets for fingerprints, IPs, and email domains, as well as adding new indexes for optimized queries. The configuration is flexible, allowing adjustments to limits based on environment settings.

items.title: Enhance async task detection capabilities in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the detection of completed asynchronous tasks by recognizing top-level `finished_at` and nested `response.success` without relying on `response.status`. The changes ensure that terminal and non-terminal statuses remain authoritative, while also aligning the null/status semantics between Python and TypeScript. Comprehensive regression tests have been added to validate these enhancements.

items.title: Enhance email security by blocking abusive domains in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a configurable policy to block specific email domains, enhancing security during user registration and email code issuance. The implementation includes checks for GitHub, Google, and Apple signups while ensuring existing users can still log in. Notably, the domain 'web-library.net' has been added to the block list due to its history of generating automated accounts.

items.title: Revamp Nexior UI for Consumer-First Approach in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update shifts the Nexior UI development to a consumer-first model, closing previous PRs that followed a pre-built scenario framework. Key changes include the prohibition of zero-consumer components and a focus on real user issues with main-based PRs. The implementation emphasizes direct fixes to real pages and ensures that any new components are only added when necessary.

items.title: Align Pika Ingredient Fields for Better Usability in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update removes the 60px offset between Pika Ingredients and Image fields, ensuring a cleaner layout. It consolidates labels and info icons into a single column with a fixed 6px gap, while also fixing issues related to image preview synchronization and upload error reporting. Overall, these changes enhance the user interface and improve functionality.

items.title: Unify Generator Field Margins for Consistency in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update aligns the margin settings of various generator fields across the Nexior project to a consistent 16px vertical rhythm. Key components modified include Fish, Sora, Hailuo, and Veo, ensuring a uniform appearance. All changes have been validated through runtime checks and code quality tools, confirming no outliers remain.

items.title: Enable OpenAI Image Fanout for Bananarouter in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enables the OpenAI Images fanout feature for the bananarouter in production by setting the environment variable `OPENAI_IMAGE_FANOUT_ENABLED=true`. The change specifically affects the `platform-service-bananarouter-openai` deployment while leaving other deployments unchanged. Validation steps confirm that the implementation is clean and functional.

items.title: Enhance Logging for Bananarouter Responses in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new logging mechanism for Bananarouter image responses, ensuring that sensitive information is omitted while preserving essential identifiers. Key changes include the addition of a sanitized summary that captures provider request IDs, response IDs, and usage counts, which will help in tracking and debugging issues related to prompt assembly. The improvements aim to close the evidence gap for provider responses, enhancing overall system reliability.

items.title: Enhance connection method schema in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `Connector.connection_methods` schema, enhancing the existing connection methods without disrupting current installations. It includes backfilling legacy connectors and connections, along with validation helpers to ensure compatibility with future runtime support. Comprehensive tests confirm the stability and integrity of the changes.

items.title: Enhance Bananarouter with Image Batch Fanout in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a prototype for batch processing of OpenAI Images on the Bananarouter channel, allowing for multiple image generations while maintaining existing functionality for single requests. Key features include improved error handling, optimized resource usage, and the ability to return successful images in order, even if some requests fail. The new feature is opt-in and disabled by default, ensuring stability in production environments.

items.title: Revamp API Documentation with Agent Workbench in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive plan for transforming API document pages into an Agent Workbench, enhancing user experience with a focus on service integration. Key changes include locking the `agent` contract, defining localization strategies, and outlining a detailed rollout plan across multiple PRs. The new structure aims to streamline access to API resources and improve clarity for users navigating through various agent services.

items.title: Revamp Connector Architecture for Enhanced Clarity in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive plan to separate the execution and credential methods within the connector architecture, moving away from the overloaded `auth_mode` and `Connection.kind`. Key changes include a full migration inventory for 115 connectors, a detailed migration strategy, and a breakdown of implementation across eight PRs. The older multi-auth plan has been marked as superseded, ensuring clarity and improved functionality moving forward.

items.title: Streamlined Generator Panel Spacing in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update unifies the sidebar spacing for 21 generator components, ensuring a consistent width of 320px for desktop and 340px for mobile drawers. Key changes include the removal of unnecessary padding and the establishment of a single scroll area for content, enhancing user experience across various services. All modifications have been validated and pass ESLint, Prettier, and type checks.

items.title: Enhance Browser Connector Recognition in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of browser connectors by ensuring they are only recognized as connected when their exact browser selection is successful for the current request. It also shares the connection status across various components, enhancing consistency and reliability. Additionally, browser sessions are now kept unavailable for token/MCP and unattended execution paths, improving security and functionality.

items.title: Fix Connector Naming in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update ensures that the browser Connector names are displayed accurately by prioritizing the joined catalog name. In cases where the catalog is unavailable, it falls back to the Connection-level identity, preventing any confusion with device names. The changes have been validated with successful checks across Vue TypeScript, ESLint, and production builds.

items.title: Enhance Audio Previews for Accessibility in Nexior 🎧
items.summary: This update transforms the audio preview component from a clickable div to a native button, improving keyboard accessibility. Users can now activate the audio previews using the Enter and Space keys, and a warning is displayed if the audio fails to load. Localization updates have also been made to ensure accessible labels are available in multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance Seedream with Explicit Generate/Edit Options in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces clear distinctions between Generate and Edit modes on the Seedream page using Element Plus radio buttons. Users can now switch between modes without losing uploaded images, and the interface disables invalid actions based on model capabilities. Additionally, inactive generation options have been cleaned up to improve user experience.

items.title: Enhance Kling Tab Readability in Nexior 📱
items.summary: This update improves the readability of the Kling scenario tabs by ensuring they remain visible in narrow parameter panels. Key changes include allowing tab labels to display in two lines and adjusting the height and line spacing for better accessibility. The modifications have been validated with passing tests and lint checks.

items.title: Update FontAwesome to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @fortawesome/fontawesome-svg-core package from version 7.2.0 to 7.3.1. Key changes include improvements and new features as noted in the release notes. The package-lock and package.json files have been modified to reflect this update.

items.title: Align Video Duration Default with OpenAPI in Clis 🎥
items.summary: This update modifies the default video duration in the Grok CLI from 8 to 6 seconds, aligning it with the OpenAPI specification. The command tests have been extended to ensure that the outgoing payload reflects this change when the duration flag is omitted. Additionally, the CLI documentation has been updated to accurately represent the new default value.

items.title: Enhance Browser Connector Installation Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the installation process for browser connectors by integrating the BrowserDevicePicker into the Browse Connectors page. Users can now select compatible devices before pairing, ensuring a smoother experience. Additionally, the Browser Devices navigation is now permanently accessible, and backend errors are surfaced in the picker for better user feedback.

items.title: Refine X402 Hosting Strategy in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update narrows the production `X402_HOSTS` to exclusively use `x402.acedata.cloud`, enhancing security by ensuring tokenless requests to the API host return a standard HTTP 400 error. Key validations confirm that the new setup passes all tests, including the full Gateway suite. The deployment is now streamlined for better performance and reliability.

items.title: Refine Veo Routing to Exclude CQTai in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes `veo31-fast-ingredients` from the CQTai capability list in the production Veo router, ensuring that requests are routed through Mountsea and TTAPI instead. Additionally, a Go regression test has been implemented to validate the changes, enhancing the reliability of the routing process. The CI workflow has also been updated to include a `go-test` job for the failover-router.

items.title: Align Task Identities with Services in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces legacy `XXX Bot` labels in task responses with each capability's Service name, ensuring consistency across the platform. Additionally, task avatars and names are now sourced from the same capability presentation, honoring display name and icon URL overrides. These changes enhance the user experience by providing a more cohesive interface.

items.title: Enhance Task History Visibility for Users in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a new task-history rule across all `/tasks` endpoints, ensuring that both grantees and owners can view the appropriate task history. The changes allow application owners to see all tasks billed to them, while grantees will only see their own tasks. This enhancement improves user experience and data visibility across the platform.

items.title: Enhance Task History Visibility for Owners and Grantees in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where task history was incorrectly displayed for grantees using shared credits. Now, both application owners and grantees can view their respective tasks accurately. The OpenAI worker's task retrieval logic has been adjusted to ensure proper scoping by user roles, enhancing clarity and usability.

items.title: Enhance X402 Host Cutoff to Fail Closed in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `/test-x402` to utilize the dedicated `x402.acedata.cloud` contract, ensuring that various checks fail closed. Key improvements include enforcing a two-PR Gateway rollout boundary and requiring explicit consent for payments. Additionally, independent review findings have been addressed to enhance the robustness of the implementation.

items.title: Update custom-auth plugin to version 0.12.0 in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update versions the Kong `custom-auth` plugin to `0.12.0`, which now includes forwarding the original request Host as `X-Request-Host`. The changes ensure that production traffic behavior remains unaffected, with thorough validation confirming the integrity of the Gateway functionality. The plugin archive has been prepared for manual activation in Tencent TSE following successful tests.

items.title: Set Default API Clients to X402 Gateway in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update defaults the Python and TypeScript API clients to use `https://x402.acedata.cloud`, while maintaining the existing management plane. Key enhancements include independent retry mechanisms for paid requests, improved streaming payment negotiation, and better error handling for malformed requests. Validation tests confirm the changes are functioning as expected.

items.title: Update FontAwesome Vue Package in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @fortawesome/vue-fontawesome package from version 3.3.0 to 3.3.2. Key improvements include new canvas sizing options and fixes for TypeScript errors in Vue render functions. This change enhances the overall functionality and stability of the icon library used in the project.
